Patch Set 2: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

Patch Set 2:

I wonder what will happen if the first server has mounted the filesystem successfully, then 2nd doesn't and reset label back to "virgin" ?
most of time 2nd won't be able to do so, because 1st is still using it, but if it umount quickly enough ?

Patch Set 2:

Alex: If the first server mount succeed, the label should be changed, so the second server mount won't change the label anymore even if mount failed.

Patch Set 2:

Niu, I'm not sure.. imagine they start both roughly at the same time and both read "virgin" label first.

Patch Set 2:

> Niu, I'm not sure.. imagine they start both roughly at the same time and both read "virgin" label first.

Maybe we need check the mmp option. If mmp is enabled, then we set label before mount, else we set label after mount like before.

In the mmp enabled case, if the second mount fails, it can not set label back because the first mount succeed and mmp will protect the first mount.

Patch Set 2:

> In the mmp enabled case, if the second mount fails, it can not set label back because the first mount succeed and mmp will protect the first mount.

imagine the first one mount and umounts immediately, not very likely, but possible.

Patch Set 2:

in general, the problem is that mount and label set isn't atomic in sense of MMP.
Uploaded patch set 3.

Patch Set 3:

> imagine the first one mount and umounts immediately, not very likely, but possible.

After looking into ext4_multi_mount_protect(), I find that even two mounts both read out EXT4_MMP_SEQ_CLEAN sequence number at the same time, one of them will finally fail _before_ the other one succeeds.

Patch Set 3:

Patch Set 3:

> After looking into ext4_multi_mount_protect(), I find that even two mounts both read out EXT4_MMP_SEQ_CLEAN sequence number at the same time, one of them will finally fail _before_ the other one succeeds.

I don't quite follow, please explain. it doesn't need to be exactly same time: one opens device, read the label, close the device. now another do the same?

Patch Set 3:

Toro has started the test process

Patch Set 3:

Toro has started the test process

Patch Set 3:

> I don't quite follow, please explain. it doesn't need to be exactly same time: one opens device, read the label, close the device. now another do the same?

I think this is a very rare case, and the result isn't fatal: if the label is changed back to 'virgin' mistakenly, it just means that next mount will have to erase the log and re-register again.

Imagine if a mount failed after successfull registration, then 'virgin' label won't be changed at all, it has the same consequence.

Patch Set 3:

> I think this is a very rare case, and the result isn't fatal: if the label is changed back to 'virgin' mistakenly, it just means that next mount will have to erase the log and re-register again. Imagine if a mount failed after successfull registration, then 'virgin' label won't be changed at all, it has the same consequence.

indeed it's rare.  the difference is that failed mount can't be access by the clients and it's just totally fine to start from scratch. but if mount succeed and the clients were able to use it, then what do we do ? what such a re-registration will mean to the clients already connected to succeed mount ? 

I'm not saying something fatal will happen immediately, but I'd like to understand the consequences better.

Patch Set 3: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

mark it -1 until we're in agreement the approach taken is safe.

Uploaded patch set 4.

Patch Set 4:

Updated the patch to use "tune2fs -f -L" instead of "e2label" in ldiskfs_label_lustre(). While "-f" option is passed to tune2fs, the mmp check will be skipped.
Patch Set 4: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

parents.subject: LU-3057 llite: swap layout fixes - hang at sanity 56x and 56w

commit.subject: LU-2008 utils: make label method work with mmp enabled target
commit.message:
LU-2008 utils: make label method work with mmp enabled target

Commit cfef795 introduced label methods for ldiskfs and zfs to
make mount utility update the label after the first successful
mount. However, if the MMP feature was enabled on an ldiskfs
target before mounting, running mount utility on the target
will fail at ldiskfs_label_lustre() because MMP will prevent
e2label from setting the label.

This patch fixes the above issue by using "tune2fs -f -L" to force
setting the label instead of using e2label in ldiskfs_label_lustre().

Test-Parameters: envdefinitions=SLOW=yes \
clientcount=4 osscount=2 mdscount=2 \
austeroptions=-R failover=true useiscsi=true \
testlist=mmp

Signed-off-by: Jian Yu <jian.yu@intel.com>
Change-Id: I6af753d1d841da6493402c153a695eb07ee7ce5b
